title: Lysozyme-based removal of bacteria from cultures of Cafeteria roenbergensis
author: Monica Berjon-Otero
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.88ihzue

steps.instruction: Determine the cell density of Cafeteria roenbergensis : stain 10 µL of a Cafeteria culture with 1 µL of Lugol’s acid iodine solution and count the cells on a haemocytometer (Neubauer Chamber)
Dilute the Cafeteria culture to 5 x 10 5 cells/mL with f/2 medium + 0.03% (w/v) yeast extract. Incubate overnight at 22-25 °C.
Determine the flagellate concentration and use approx. 8 x 10 7 Cafeteria cells/sample
Centrifuge flagellates at 4,500 g, 5 min, room temperature in 50 mL Falcon tubes. Flagellates will be in the pellet.
Resuspend the cell pellets in 1.8 mL of 1X PBS and transfer them into 2 mL microfuge tubes.

Add 40 μL of 50 mg/mL freshly prepared lysozyme in 1X PBS (1 mg/mL final concentration). Incubate for 1 hour at room temperature. Optionally, you can also add 5 mM EDTA here. EDTA increases the activity of lysozyme against Gram negative bacteria, but also negatively affects Cafeteria mobility.

Add 200 µL of 10 mg/mL freshly prepared DNaseI in 1X PBS (1 mg/mL final concentration, 2 U/µL) and 11 µL of 1M MgCl 2 (5 mM final concentration). Incubate for 30 min at 34°C in a thermomixer with shaking.
Resuspend the pellets in 100 µL of 1X gradient buffer (0.5 M NaCl in 1X PBS). Pipette up and down several times to break up aggregates.
Up to 1 x 10 9 Cafeteria cells can be loaded per SW28 centrifuge tube. Add 1X gradient buffer up to 0.5 - 1 mL final resuspended cell volume.

Load the sample on top of a two-step Optiprep cushion cushion, 20% (w/v) Optiprep at the bottom of the tube and 10% (w/v) Optiprep on top of the 20% layer (both Optiprep solutions are prepared in 1X gradient buffer).
Centrifuge the tubes in an ultracentrifuge, Beckman SW28 rotor, at 20,000 g, 20 min, 20°C, with slow braking.
Recover Cafeteria cells from the gradient, at the interphase between the 10% and 20% Optiprep layers.
Dilute the recovered Cafeteria cells at least twofold with F/2 medium (no yeast extract)

Diluite the culture 25fold or 50fold with F/2 and stain 10 µL of this dilution with 1 µL of Lugol’s acid iodine solution Load the sample on a haemocytometer to quantify Cafeteria cells and remaining bacteria.

Dilute Cafeteria cells to the desired density. If no or little dilution is required, you can centrifuge again for 6 min at 5,000 g, 20°C and resuspend the cells in F/2 to remove all the remaining Optiprep.
